text: You’ll help translate data into growth by shaping how we acquire, engage, and convert users, contributing directly to the evolution of our products and go-to-market strategies. Talent Transformation is the strategic evolution of skills, capabilities, and associated organisational wiring that support an organisation’s business strategy. It's about continuously reinventing talent by identifying and executing on the most critical changes needed across the enterprise, for teams, and for individuals. This is more than just training people, finding faster ways to hire people, or implementing a technology. It’s about truly transforming an organisation’s talent and the systems that create lasting organisational change to accelerate strategic execution, drive speed and agility, amplify talent potential, build resilience, and improve sustainability.

We partner with organisations to build a talent strategy that defines how to access, create, and unlock the potential of talent. We help access talent by strengthening diverse talent pipelines, both internal and external, matching the needs of the business to people’s skills and aspirations. We help create talent by using skills as the foundation to help people continuously build market relevant skills through equitable access to career opportunities. And we unlock potential by creating conditions across the organisation that allow people to do their best work every day and leave them net better off.

We view skills-driven organisations as a human + technology network where skill-based talent practices unlock opportunities for individuals and maximise talent potential for the organisation. We support our clients to build the right capability through implementing learning and re-skilling programmes to ensure organisations have the right people and right skills to better position their talent across the organisation based on skill supply/demand.

Work in dynamic teams to deliver innovative learning and reskilling solutions to our clients. Work with cutting-edge learning technologies and have the opportunity to develop a wide range of new skills on the job. If you’re looking for a challenging career, working in a vibrant environment with access to training and a global network of learning consultants, this could be the role for you.

Challenge our clients to think differently in how they can ensure they power their people today and ready them for tomorrow with the right skills and capabilities, e.g.. by supporting clients to transition to become a Skills Based Organisation (SBO), fostering a more adaptable, efficient, and innovative workforce with greater internal mobility
Lead global delivery teams to deliver workforce strategy and transformation, talent strategy, learning, and reskilling projects at our core clients
Own client relationships and help to lead our clients to deliver leading Talent Transformation initiatives.
Empower Analysts and Consultants to deliver at their full potential through coaching, mentoring and people leadership.
Help to grow Talent Transformation by getting involved in sales processes, including contributing to bid submissions and client presentations.
Understand how digital and AI are impacting the workforce and how required transformational change will mean new skills and roles to tap future value
Support organisations to understand the skills they have, the skills they need and how best to address their skill gaps
Deliver Talent, learning and reskilling strategies and projects to build the right capability for our clients

Support client to future proof their workforce planning through Strategic Workforce Planning to support the alignment of an organisation's workforce with its business goals by ensuring they have the right people, with the right skills, in the right roles, at the right time to meet their future strategic needs
Support clients to rethink ways of working and the behaviours needed to equip their teams to deliver results quickly
Supporting complex and large-scale Technology transformation programmes to implement an impactful learning and skilling experience

Help clients to build a Skills Architecture, to define, map, and manage its workforce's skill landscape, connecting them to roles, career paths, and business goals. The provides clients with actionable intelligence for strategic workforce planning, talent mobility, and aligns the workforce with business needs
Be versed on Technology that underpins Talent engagements (LXP, LMS, etc.), to be able to advise clients on appropriate tooling
We are looking for experience in the following skills:
Proven track record in delivering talent management and capability strategy, programmes and/or processes.
Ability to conduct capability gap assessments and/or training and learning needs analysis within organisations
Implementing learning and/or reskilling programmes to build capability within organisations
A familiarity or interest in the relevant technologies for pursuing re-skilling from innovative learning to skills analytics
Strong consulting acumen by demonstrating excellent communication, facilitation and data analysis skills to influence others.
Showing ability to build trusted relationships, and manage stakeholders, both internally and externally to your organisation with clear ability to influence others.
Are forward thinking with a strong business acumen
Have experience in leading global teams in complex learning implementations
Can guide and coach other team members to achieve their full potential

What You’ll Bring

Strong understanding of key HR data concepts and processes across the employee lifecycle.
Demonstrated experience in data governance, data modelling, cleansing, and building scalable analytics environments.
Exceptional analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to turn raw data into actionable insights and compelling narratives.
Strong communication and stakeholder-management skills, with the capability to present complex analyses to non-technical audiences.

Join the DataAnnotation team and contribute to developing cutting-edge AI systems, while enjoying the flexibility of remote work and setting your own schedule.

We are looking for experienced quantitative professionals to help advance AI development. AI models are increasingly capable of performing complex analytical and scientific reasoning — but these systems still need practitioners with real-world quantitative experience to validate whether the outputs actually hold up in practice.

As a member of DataAnnotation's team, you'll work closely with state-of-the-art AI models on tasks like evaluating AI-generated quantitative analysis, solving technical problems, and providing feedback that directly shapes how these systems reason about data, models, and scientific problems. Whether your background is in data science, astrophysics, economics, biostatistics, operations research, or any other quantitative field, if you think rigorously about data and models, your skills are directly applicable here. Some team members fit this work alongside a full-time role, while others treat it as their primary focus.
Evaluate AI-generated quantitative work, including statistical analysis, predictive modeling, scientific reasoning, and data-driven insights, for technical accuracy and real-world validity.
Design and solve quantitative problems used to train and benchmark AI systems, spanning areas like forecasting, experimental analysis, optimization, and statistical inference.
Write clear technical explanations and well-documented analytical code.
Provide feedback that directly shapes the next generation of AI models built for quantitative reasoning.

2+ years of hands-on experience in a quantitative role or research environment — such as data science, statistics, economics, finance, physics, biology, epidemiology, operations research, or any adjacent field.
Some coding experience required, with comfort writing and reviewing analytical code end-to-end.
Practical experience with statistical methods, predictive modeling, and experiment design (e.g., A/B testing, hypothesis testing, regression, classification, time-series forecasting).
Fluency in English (native or bilingual level) with strong writing skills.
A bachelor's degree in a quantitative field is preferred (Statistics, Computer Science, Mathematics, Engineering, or similar); a master's or PhD is a plus.

We’re looking for a People Data Analyst to join the global HR Operations Team and help us build the foundation of people analytics at FareHarbor. Proven ability to build reporting frameworks and dashboards from the ground up, establishing structure and standards where none exist.
Self-starter with a passion for scaling people analytics from foundational reporting to advanced insights and data storytelling.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with excellent attention to detail and data accuracy.

Supporting the smooth running and augmentation of business operations through the provision of insight on metrics that matter

Practical experience in a business analytics setting, preferably in a fast-paced, dynamic environment

We are looking for a People Analytics Analyst to join our recently formed People Analytics team and serve as a strategic, data-driven partner to leaders across the business. This role blends strong analytical and technical skills with a consultative, business-facing mindset — someone who loves working with data and using it to influence decisions.

This role will partner closely with HR Business Partners, the People Leadership Team (PLT), senior business leaders, and teams across the broader People organization (e.g., Talent Acquisition, L&D, Total Rewards, Workday), translating complex people data into clear insights, narratives, and recommendations. A significant focus of this role will be supporting Sales and Go-To-Market (GTM) organizations, using workforce data to help leaders understand performance, retention, hiring, and organizational effectiveness within revenue-generating teams. You will report to the Director of People Analytics.

The People Analytics function is entering its next phase of growth — expanding beyond foundational reporting into deeper insights, standardization, and proactive workforce analytics. This role will play a critical part in shaping how people data is used to understand organizational health, guide strategy, and support leaders in solving real business problems.

If you’re excited about being both a hands-on data practitioner and a trusted advisor to the business, particularly in fast-paced, performance-driven environments like Sales, we’d love to hear from you.

The Business Architect's architecture is the "connective tissue" that integrates strategies across multiple programs and projects and ensures alignment with business strategies and outcomes. This "connective tissue" is a series of explicit graphical models that, at a minimum, define and describe the goals the business is trying to achieve, the processes needed to be performed to meet these goals, the data needed to perform the processes, and the roles and responsibilities required to perform the process activities. Architects design various processes and documents that companies use to pursue their core values, align business practices and execute plans. These architects produce supporting documents, such as maps, models, process diagrams and presentations, to communicate their ideas and advise others. They provide support and guidance in consultations that assess a business's capabilities, establish goals and communicate designs to clients, executives and other managers. This multifaceted job profile requires technical proficiencies and interpersonal skills.
Knowledge and Skills Desired
Planning and analysis experience including modeling graphical representation and abstractions
Systems and/or supply applications background (10+ years) with proficiency in learning software quickly
Advanced communication skills, catered to a wide variety and levels of audiences

Strong understanding of how to apply policies and procedures to business process transformation
Advanced multi-tasking, problem-solving, and prioritization skills
Advanced consulting, influence, and negotiation skills
Group session facilitation
Product and Corporate Compliance, Environmental and Social Impact, and/or ESG domain/function knowledge, expertise, and experience

Effective Communication
Results Orientation

Customer Centricity

Typically has 5+ years of experience in direct/indirect people leadership.

Change & Stakeholder Management: You will act as a key bridge between HR teams, IT and external providers. You will support change management activities related to technology and AI, fostering adoption and sustainable ways of working.

We are looking for a People Analytics Specialist to join our Human Resources team. In this role you will help shape a more data‑driven HR function by transforming complex workforce data into clear insights that support decision‑making across the company. You will work with HR leadership and cross‑functional teams to build reliable reporting, strengthen data quality, and support the development of our people‑related processes. If you enjoy combining analytics with practical business impact and appreciate an environment that moves quickly, this role will suit you well.

You will lead and develop our HR data strategy and make sure we work with accurate, well‑structured and reliable data. You will turn workforce information into insights that support planning, reporting and decision‑making. This includes maintaining data quality, driving compliance processes and ensuring strong foundations for headcount management and HR KPIs.
Implementing and maintaining a data‑driven HR function with high data quality
Owning the full HR data lifecycle and securing a clear and reliable single source of truth
Setting and managing data compliance routines in line with GDPR and internal policies

Translating workforce data into insights for HR leadership and business partners

The ideal candidate

You bring an analytical and structured approach and enjoy solving problems where data is central. You are naturally curious about new tools and technology and feel confident working with both data and people.

Understanding of GDPR and data privacy processes related to personnel data
Strong ability to translate headcount and workforce information into concise updates that support leaders in their decision‑making

As a Senior Solution Architect in the Services team at Fivetran, you will facilitate the implementation, configuration, and optimization of the Fivetran platform and product suite through your expertise in various applications, databases, data warehouses / lakehouses, and APIs. This includes adjacent transformation, open data infrastructure (e.g., open storage and table formats), and data activation workflows where relevant. Working directly with customer stakeholders, you will define requirements and work to facilitate an architecture and integration that fulfills those requirements. Along the way, you will facilitate the optimization of the implementation through performance tuning, offer best-practice advice on source and target setup & use your analytical skills to troubleshoot issues and support use cases where trusted, well-governed data pipelines enable analytics and emerging AI/ML initiatives. An essential part of the role of the Senior Solution Architect will be enabling customers to take responsibility for the installation once it has been tested and is ready for deployment into a Production environment. To this end, you should feel comfortable in producing enablement collateral and training teams to adopt what may be a new software application.
Fivetran
Fivetran SaaS, Hybrid Deployment, and HVR (self-hosted) architectures
On-premises and cloud databases, including: Oracle Microsoft SQL Server IBM Db2 MySQL PostgreSQL SAP HANA
On-premises and cloud data warehouses / lakehouses, including:: Snowflake Databricks BigQuery Redshift
Fivetran Managed Data Lake Service (MDLS) concepts and open table formats such as Apache Iceberg and Delta Lake
Operating systems, including: Windows Linux Unix
Cloud Services Providers AWS, GCP or Azure environments and services
Container and Orchestration Docker, Podman, and Kubernetes (including containerized application deployment and orchestration) Git
As a key member of our customer-facing team, you’ll be directly involved in helping customers successfully implement and optimize Fivetran data movement solutions across various data environments.
Installation and Setup - Lead the installation and setup of Fivetran products for customers, ensuring that environments are fully prepared for replication.
This will involve: Preparing environments for replication, including SQL scripting or command-line operations to ensure full transaction logging, archiving/backup, and table analysis/preparation.
Configuring high-volume database connectors using user interfaces (UIs), command-line tools, and/or APIs, to meet customer needs.

Work in containerized environments, including Kubernetes, with hands-on involvement in deployment, configuration, and troubleshooting, while collaborating with infrastructure or DevOps teams as needed.
Implementation Support and Troubleshooting - Work closely with customer teams to identify, troubleshoot, and resolve any issues during the implementation phase.
You will focus on: Assisting with data validation to ensure accurate data replication. Analyzing and optimizing performance to meet customer expectations.
Offering database and environment tuning to enhance overall efficiency and effectiveness.

Customer Training and Enablement Empower customer teams to take full ownership of Fivetran implementations by providing hands-on training, detailed documentation, and ongoing support, ensuring they feel confident managing their data pipelines independently.

Extensive Customer-Facing Experience 8+ years of extensive experience in customer-facing teams, you will be directly involved in helping customers successfully implement and optimize data integrations using the Fivetran platform and product suite across various environments.

Data Integration and Replication - A solid understanding of the data integration and replication space, with a proven ability to explain complex technical concepts to customers in a clear, approachable manner.

Broad Technical Knowledge - Experience working with a wide range of databases, data warehouses / lakehouses, and operating systems, enabling you to support customers across various environments and ensure their systems are optimized for Fivetran’s solutions.
Advanced SQL and Command-Line Skills - Strong proficiency in SQL and command-line scripting, allowing you to confidently guide customers through complex technical setups and troubleshoot issues.

Networking and Connectivity - Advanced understanding of networking approaches, including SSH tunneling, VPNs, and PrivateLink / private connectivity (AWS, Azure, GCP), ensuring you can work with customers to configure secure, efficient data replication solutions.

Prioritization and Organization - Demonstrated ability to prioritize and organize work, ensuring that customer needs are met effectively and in a timely manner, even when juggling multiple requests or tasks.
Programming and Automation - Familiarity with scripting and automation languages such as Python to support customers in automating processes or resolving issues efficiently.

Experience with enterprise platforms such as SAP or IBM iSeries

We build connected, AI-powered systems that understand customer context, personalize experiences, and guide users toward the next best action. From onboarding to everboarding, we focus on helping customers achieve value faster and continuously over time.
We’re looking for a Principal Software Engineer to help define and build the future of the Customer Journey at HubSpot.

This is a deeply hands-on role focused on building intelligent, scalable systems that power onboarding, activation, and long-term customer success. You’ll shape the architecture behind our Everboarding engine — a system that consumes millions of signals to predict next best actions, power surfaces like Global Home, and drive smarter growth and upgrade experiences.

Customer Journey defines how every customer experiences HubSpot. The systems you build will determine how quickly customers find value, how effectively they grow, and how intelligently the product adapts to their needs.
As a Principal Engineer, you’ll go beyond execution — you’ll define direction, turn ambiguity into simple, scalable systems, and shape the core experience of HubSpot for millions of users.

Build intelligent, AI-powered customer experiences Design and ship systems that personalize the customer journey using real-time signals and predictions. Help evolve the Activate and Everboarding engines to drive next-best actions, onboarding flows, and growth opportunities.

Own platform-level architecture Shape the technical direction across onboarding, activation, and engagement systems. Define reusable patterns for personalization, decisioning, experimentation, and orchestration at scale.
Be a hands-on, high-leverage builder Write production code and lead complex initiatives end-to-end. Take ideas from early prototypes to systems used by millions of customers.

Drive simplicity in complex systems Create clear, consistent models across lifecycle stages, onboarding states, and signals. Reduce fragmentation and make the experience intuitive across the platform.
Raise the bar across the org Mentor senior engineers, lead design reviews, and influence technical decisions across teams. Act as a multiplier who improves how teams build and ship.
What You Bring
Proven experience building and scaling distributed systems used by millions of users.
Strong architectural instincts with a bias toward quick iteration, simplicity, clarity, and extensibility.
Experience applying ML or GenAI in real products, with a healthy skepticism for “magic” and a bias toward trust and control.
A track record of owning and delivering large, and complex cross-team initiatives.
Comfort operating in ambiguity and turning it into clear plans and working systems.
A hands-on mindset — you lead by building, not just by guiding.
Strong product intuition and a focus on delivering real customer value.

Role Overview:  The Senior Director, AI Systems will serve as the strategic and technical cornerstone of our Core AI Agentic Development Team. This leadership role is responsible for overseeing all AI development in production and ensuring that our multi-agent systems deliver tangible value in optimizing healthcare revenue lifecycles. The Senior Director will partner closely with the product management team to shape the AI roadmap, prioritize initiatives, and determine the most appropriate AI technologies and agent architectures for specific business challenges and opportunities.
Responsibilities:
Define and drive the enterprise AI strategy, aligning multi-agent system development with business objectives to optimize healthcare revenue cycle performance.
Lead architecture decisions for large-scale, production-grade agentic systems, including multi-agent orchestration, oracle frameworks, and enterprise system integration.
Partner with product leadership to shape the AI roadmap, prioritize initiatives, and select the most effective technologies and approaches for complex business challenges.
Establish and enforce governance, compliance, and guardrail frameworks to ensure safe, auditable, and regulatory-compliant AI system behavior (e.g., HIPAA alignment).
Oversee end-to-end delivery and performance of AI systems in production, defining KPIs and monitoring ROI, reliability, and operational impact.
Build, scale, and lead high-performing AI engineering teams, including budget management, resource planning, and organizational development.
Provide cross-functional leadership across engineering, product, and operations to translate business needs into scalable AI architectures and solutions.

Required Experience and Skills  Candidates must have a substantial background in leading AI development teams and successfully delivering complex, agentic AI systems into production environments at scale. This role requires a deep strategic understanding of the current AI landscape, specifically focusing on agent orchestration, governance, and system integration.

Strategic Agent Architecture:  Proven ability to evaluate and select the right architectures for complex problems, particularly focusing on multi-agent orchestration,  oracle agents , and the integration of  voice AI  and  computer use  capabilities to drive existing enterprise systems.

Governance & Compliance:  Deep expertise in establishing  agent governance  frameworks and robust  guardrails . The candidate must ensure that autonomous systems operate safely, predictably, and in compliance with strict industry regulations.

Cross-Functional Leadership:  Exceptional collaboration skills, particularly in working alongside product teams to translate business needs into technical strategies and providing authoritative guidance on technology selection.

Operational Excellence:  Proven capability in managing engineering budgets, scaling technical teams, and understanding the operational metrics required to monitor the ROI, performance, and reliability of production AI systems.

Preferred Qualifications  Extensive experience within the healthcare revenue lifecycle sector is highly sought after. A deep comprehension of healthcare provider workflows, accounts receivable optimization, and revenue cycle management—combined with knowledge of healthcare compliance (e.g., HIPAA)—will enable the Senior Director to drive highly targeted and effective AI strategies that directly impact the bottom line of healthcare organizations.
### The role

The Hartford is seeking a highly skilled, hands-on AI Architect to support the Enterprise Technology Architecture (ETA) organization.

This role will be responsible for leading the design, governance, and implementation of AI-centric technology architectures across a hybrid infrastructure landscape , including AWS, Google Cloud Platform (GCP), and on-premises data centers .

The AI Architect will play a critical role in enabling the responsible and secure adoption of Generative AI (GenAI) technologies , establishing architectural standards, and driving the implementation of multiple internal-facing GenAI use cases .
This role requires a strong blend of strategic architectural background and hands-on technical execution .
Key Responsibilities: Build and implement Generative AI and Agentic AI capabilities that enhance internal business processes and customer experiences.
Develop and govern AI agents and copilots, including experience with Microsoft Copilot Studio and enterprise agent development patterns.
Design and develop Agentic AI solutions leveraging Model Context Protocol (MCP), Google Agent Development Kit (ADK), LangGraph / LangChain, and Agent Engine on Google Cloud Platform (GCP).
Ensure AI and GenAI solutions are architecturally aligned with enterprise technology strategy, and meet scalability, security, resiliency, and compliance requirements.
Drive enterprise adoption of GenAI and Agentic AI frameworks, establishing reusable patterns, reference architectures, and best practices.
Lead and execute proofs of concept (POCs) to evaluate emerging AI technologies, tools, and frameworks, and transition successful POCs into production-ready solutions.
Collaborate closely with Enterprise Architects, Platform Engineering, Security, and Data teams to ensure AI solutions align with approved reference architectures and governance standards.
Stay current with industry trends in Generative AI, agent frameworks, and AI platforms, proactively recommending innovative and practical solutions for enterprise use cases.
Familiarity with security and privacy controls for AI/ML workloads to protect against exploitation of data or models during training, validation and inference.
Managing security policy and drift detection at scale across the enterprise.
Cloud Security (AWS & GCP) Architect and implement secure cloud solutions leveraging native services and third-party tools.
Define and enforce cloud security posture management (CSPM), identity and access management (IAM), secrets, key management and encryption strategies.
Collaborate with DevOps and cloud engineering teams to embed security into foundational networking components, CI/CD pipelines and infrastructure-as-code.
Datacenter & Hybrid Security Ensure secure integration between cloud platforms and on-prem datacenters, including network segmentation, VPNs, and secure data flows.
Oversee security controls for legacy systems and their modernization paths.
GenAI Security Enablement Define security and governance frameworks for GenAI platforms and use cases.
Ensure responsible AI practices including data privacy, model integrity, explainability and ethical AI usage.
Collaborate with AI/ML teams to secure model training, inference, and deployment pipelines.

Governance & Collaboration Serve as a key member of the Enterprise Technology & Solution Governance Partner with business, IT, and risk stakeholders to align security architecture with enterprise goals.
Provide technical guidance and mentorship to junior engineers and architects on AI development practices.
Required Qualifications: Experience : 10-12 years in Engineering / Architecture disciplines, with at least 2+ years in GenAI and Agentic AI development.
Project Delivery : Must have at least one GenAI or Agentic AI project end-to-end delivery experience.
Technical Expertise : Strong proficiency in Google ADK, LangGraph/Langchain, Agent Engine, RAG, and Vertex AI.
Hands-on experience with GCP services: Cloud Run, ECS, Vertex AI Search Engine, IAM, and networking.

Experience developing IaC and application pipelines using modern automation and DevOps tooling (e.g., Terraform, GitHub Actions) Solid understanding of GenAI patterns, LLM fine-tuning, and prompt engineering.
Programming Skills : Python, Java, or similar languages for AI development.
Cloud Certifications : GCP Professional Machine Learning Engineer or GCP Professional Cloud Architect preferred.
Education : Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, AI/ML, or related field.
Soft Skills : Strong problem-solving, communication, and collaboration skills.
Key Competencies: Strategic and analytical thinking Successfully integrated AI agents into business or technical workflows for automation and enhanced decision-making.
Improved operational efficiency and customer experience through AI-driven innovation.
Established reusable AI patterns and best practices for enterprise adoption.

You’ll join the team primarily responsible for Pages and Apps, where you’ll build complete product features as well as tooling and libraries to enable other engineers to ship faster, higher quality software. However, we’re still a small but growing software engineering organization, so this role is not strictly limited to any single part of our product or stack.

This role is about more than writing code: you'll have an influential voice in shaping our technical direction, our product, and our culture. We have a hugely supportive and collaborative engineering team that takes great pride in the work we do and the challenges we solve. We’re eager to help grow and nurture each other’s abilities and also create an environment where we have the ability to do our very best work.
Work closely with colleagues to build the right solutions to the problems our customers face.
Make valuable changes in our codebase in your first week, and see them live in production minutes after merging them.
Write and review high quality, well tested code to ensure that we’re creating a stable product for our customers, and a stress-free codebase for ourselves.
Identify areas for improvement, both in our codebase and in how we work, and share the responsibility of making our team better.
Own the problems you’re solving - and the software you write to solve them - all the way to production and beyond, responding to customer feedback to improve those solutions.
Shape and evolve our design system, focusing on building high-quality components that are robust, performant, and reliable, with an emphasis on thoughtful design and architecture.
At higher levels of this role, you will also:
Mentor other engineers in our team, providing support and guidance to help them grow their abilities.
Help prioritise our roadmap, identifying how we can use our time pragmatically and effectively.
Projects you might work on:
Collaborating with our Product and Design teams to build out entire product features, like apps and page collections from early tech plans to final UI fixes.
Working on Tines navigation and overall user experience.
Owning areas in our design system and working closely with our designers to create a high quality component library for all teams to use.
Improving AI tools that help engineers work more effectively with our design system.

You’ll need to be capable with a high-level programming language. Our interview process includes building a small command-line program that makes decisions based on some JSON input, so you should feel very comfortable working at that level of technical complexity. Our main languages are Ruby and TypeScript, but you don’t have to be familiar with those - we value curiosity and an eagerness to learn over existing experience. We’ll also cover the cost of education material to bring you up to speed.

This role is broad, not specialised. In addition to our frontend stack, you’ll work across our backend and infrastructure. You don’t need to have expertise in all of these areas (no one on the team does), but you will need to be comfortable pitching in anywhere when needed, with the support of your teammates. This allows us to be more effective as a team, and provides a lot of opportunities for you to learn.

The People & Culture Team plays a pivotal role in the strategy by developing a digitally skilled workforce through career pathways, continuous learning, and capacity-building initiatives to ensure UNICEF staff can effectively leverage emerging technologies and support digital transformation.

The Talent Specialist will join our team to support the Digital Impact Division in strengthening its talent and learning ecosystem. The role acts as the focal point for all learning and development work for the Division, ensuring that learning initiatives are designed and delivered in a clear and practical way. It focuses on talent development, digital learning and knowledge management, including emerging technologies such as AI. The Specialist supports the development of the capabilities needed for digital impact and transformation across the digital impact community, working closely with the Global Learning Centre (GLC) and contributing to upskilling efforts for the Global Digital Advisors (TeamOne). He/she will support learning and talent development priorities by leveraging workforce and skills insights to inform succession planning and the design of learning pathways for future roles. He/she will work closely with chiefs, managers, Human Resources Business Partners, Subject Matter Experts, regional and country offices, principal Digital Advisors, as well as collaborating with GLC to ensure alignment and relevance.

Education: Master or equivalent (Advanced University Degree) in Human Resources Management, Education, Human Resources, Organizational Development, Learning and Development, Education, Knowledge Management, International Development, or any other relevant fields if complemented with Human-Centered Design, Design Thinking, Learning Technologies, Human Development and Growth and Coaching Certification.

Work Experience: At least 5 years of relevant work experience in Human Resources, Talent Development, Learning & Development, Organizational Development, experience designing and delivering blended, digital, and self-paced learning, needs assessment, learning experience design, learning and knowledge platforms usage, and any other related fields.
Skills:
Design and delivery of talent development and learning solutions using human centered design, instructional design, and adult learning principles across virtual and in person modalities;
Stakeholder collaboration across functions and regions in multicultural environments;
Facilitation, interpersonal skills, and clear communication to translate complexity;
Data organization, analysis, and reporting to inform learning and talent decisions;
Experience coordinating external learning vendors (for example Microsoft);
Ideation and innovation.
Language Requirements: Fluency in English is required.
Knowledge of another official UN language (Arabic, Chinese, French, Russian or Spanish);
AI fluency and responsible use, including AI literacy and learning applications;
Experience with digital learning and knowledge platforms such as LMS and SharePoint;
SharePoint content management and knowledge curation;
Experience working in international or UN system organizations;
Relevant experience at country level, particularly in development, fragile settings and humanitarian contexts.

At Utopia, we’re building more than properties — we’re crafting extraordinary experiences. Founded in 2023, our in-house architectural firm, Utopia Design, is at the heart of our mission to create a global portfolio of luxurious, high-end hospitality destinations. As part of a growing real estate development group, Utopia Design brings bold vision and innovative thinking to every project we take on.
We are looking for a technically strong and detail-oriented architect to join our design team within the real estate development business, focusing on boutique hospitality assets.

In this role, you will be responsible for translating the design intent into fully coordinated, precise, and buildable documentation. You will work across all design stages with a focus on technical development - from RIBA Stage 3 through construction, ensuring that the project’s quality, constructability, and coordination are preserved throughout. You will collaborate with the broader design and engineering team and serve as a key point of contact for design problem-solving, system integration, and detailing.

We expect you to be highly proficient in Revit (including parametric families, freeform modeling, and terrain modeling), with strong BIM coordination experience. You should also have a solid understanding of construction methodologies, materials, detailing, and multi-disciplinary integration. Experience with Navisworks is required; Rhino, AutoCAD, and Enscape are a plus.

To succeed in this role, you should be independent, analytical, proactive in solving technical and coordination challenges, and capable of consistently delivering high-quality, buildable design packages. Fluency in English is essential.
Deliver technical design for high-end hospitality projects, ensuring buildability and coordination
Develop and manage coordinated Revit models and construction documentation
Coordinate architecture with structural, MEP, and interiors using BIM / Navisworks
Resolve multidisciplinary clashes and propose practical technical solutions
Prepare tender and construction-stage documentation aligned with codes and specifications
Maintain quality control of models and drawings; flag risks early
Support façade and system detailing based on construction logic
Collaborate with procurement on technical specifications and materials
Guide BIM modelers and contribute to internal technical standards and workflows
Requirements
Bachelor's or Master's Degree in Architecture
5 to 10 years of experience in architectural design
Experience working on low-rise residential and hospitality projects - private homes and villas, hotels and resorts
Excellent communication and presentation skills
Proficiency in Revit, AutoCAD and Nawisworks
Professional level of English, both written and verbal
